ES1! · CME · RTH PLAN · FRIDAY JUN 12, 2026 · UPDATED 06:29 ET (PRE-OPEN)

ES RTH Plan — Updated for Overnight Ratification

Charts: TPO with ON levels (06:27) · 30-min RTH+ETH (06:29) · Prior plan baseline: spike base 7,403.50 / yClose 7,394.50
Last 7,430.75–7,432.50 (+35.75 / +0.48%)
ON range 7,374.25 → 7,451.50
ON value: VAH 7,421.50 · POC 7,409.00 · VAL 7,391.25
Entire ON value ABOVE yClose 7,394.50
Single print 7,387 FILLED ✓ · 7,337 still open
20-EMA ≈7,434 — at price
ATR 105.50
▲ What the overnight did (vs last night's plan) The ETH played the script almost exactly: N2 extension first — through 7,424.50 to ON HIGH 7,451.50 (tagging the 7,448.50 shelf and rejecting); then N3 spike-failure repair — the dip filled the 7,387 single print and overshot to ON LOW 7,374.25, holding above 7,366.25; then the buy-the-fill response drove it back to 7,430s. Net result: a full overnight value distribution (7,391.25 / 7,409.00 / 7,421.50) built ENTIRELY above yesterday's close — that is value ratification of the Iran spike. The 7,387 ladder warning is cleared; the 7,337 single print remains the lone untested air below. New chart annotations extracted: upper stack lines 7,565.00 (red), 7,578.25, 7,591.50, 7,603.50 + an upper fib extend capping the stack — your roadmap toward the ATH zone. Price opens the morning pressing the 20-EMA (~7,434) between ON VAH 7,421.50 and ON HIGH 7,451.50.

01Top-Down — re-based at 7,430.75

a) Daily context

ReferenceValueDistanceStatus
20-EMA (est.)≈7,433–7,436≈0AT PRICE — the battle is happening right now; ON already poked through it to 7,451.50 once and was rejected
50-EMA≈7,300+131Support inside the Jun-11 POC ZONE
100-EMA≈7,144+287Deep support
ATR105.50—ON used ~77 pts; a directional RTH still has roughly ⅔ ATR of budget

Read: Two consecutive daily reversals up; today's open will print essentially ON the 20-EMA. A daily close above ~7,436 ends the correction formally and re-opens the upper stack (7,448.50 → 7,469.50 → 7,491.50 → 7,508 → 7,527.50/7,532 → your new lines 7,565 / 7,578.25 / 7,591.50 / 7,603.50 → ATH 7,632.25). A Friday close back under 7,394.50 would print a failed-reclaim wick into the weekend — the bears' only decent outcome from here.

b) 30-Min — overnight flow & updated levels

c) Market Profile — overnight detail

MetricValueRead
ON HIGH / LOW7,451.50 / 7,374.25Both-sided 77-pt night; both probes rejected back to the middle
ON VAH / POC / VAL7,421.50 / 7,409.00 / 7,391.25Entire ON value above yClose 7,394.50 — the market accepted the spike and built value on top of it
Spike base7,403.50Now embedded INSIDE ON value, just under ON POC — upgraded from "binary line" to "value-backed support"
Single print 7,387FILLED overnightRepair complete; 7,383–7,391 now tested, two-way traded structure
Single print 7,337STILL OPENThe only untested air left below — only in play under 7,366/7,350
Open projection~7,420–7,440Opens above ON POC, at/above ON VAH, ON the 20-EMA — bullish template with a balanced (not one-sided) ON inventory
UPDATED STRUCTURE MAP (pre-open) 7,603.50 / 7,591.50 / 7,578.25 / 7,565.00 ── NEW upper stack + fib cap 7,527.50–7,532 ── cluster 7,508.25 / 7,491.50 / 7,469.50 ── shelf ladder 7,448.50 / 7,451.50 ── shelf + ON HIGH — first wall 7,433–36 ── daily 20-EMA ◄ YOU ARE HERE (7,430.75) 7,421.50 / 7,424.50 ── ON VAH + pivot 7,409.00 ── ON POC 7,403.50 ── spike base (value-backed now) 7,396.50–7,391.25 ── fib + yClose + ON VAL — polarity band 7,387 / 7,383.25 ── filled print + cyan 7,374.25 ── ON LOW 7,366.25 ── teal 7,350 / 7,348.25 ── fib + yVAH ◄ deep-repair buy 7,337 ── OPEN single print ░ air ░ 7,320 → 7,294–7,307 POC ZONE → 7,262 …

Profile narrative: Overnight did the two healthiest things a spike can experience: it probed higher (proving demand above), and it repaired its own thin structure (filling 7,387, finding responsive buyers at 7,374.25) — then built a fat, symmetric value distribution on top of yesterday's close. This is textbook ratification. The auction question for RTH is no longer "is the spike real" — it's "can price accept above the 20-EMA / 7,448.50–7,451.50 wall." Failure there produces rotations back through a now well-structured 7,374–7,430 zone (buyable); acceptance opens a clean ladder to 7,469.50/7,491.50 with your new upper-stack lines mapping the path to the ATH zone.

VIX/macro carry-over: No updated VIX chart — last 20.42 falling. Iran deal still unsigned, signing "maybe over the weekend": the 2 PM Friday de-risk rule from yesterday's plan stays in force. Confirm VIX sub-20.5 at the bell for the full bull template.

03Context Into the Friday Open

DimensionReadingImplication
Overnight characterBoth-sided ratification: probe up (7,451.50 rejected), repair down (7,374.25 bought), value built above yCloseHealthiest possible follow-through to a spike close — bull template confirmed
Open location~7,425–7,435 — above ON POC, at ON VAH/20-EMAOpen at the decision level; first 30 minutes vs 7,424.50/7,436 sets the day
ON inventoryBalanced (both directions traded; late longs from 7,390s)No forced inventory correction owed — unlike yesterday; dips are choice, not necessity
Structural repair status7,387 print filled ✓ · 7,374–7,430 two-way tested · 7,337 print still openSupport below is REAL now; the air pocket only matters under 7,350
Value relationshipsON value 7,391.25–7,421.50 entirely above yCloseHigher value = trend confirmation; RTH value overlapping-higher extends it
ATR budget105.5; ON used 77Full-bull RTH ≈ 7,490–7,510 reachable; full-bear ≈ 7,340–7,360 — both map to your levels
Event clockFriday; Iran signing "maybe this weekend," unsigned2 PM de-risk rule active; MOC direction = weekend-positioning tell

04What-If Scenarios — RTH Friday Jun 12 (updated)

G1 · Dip-Buy at ON POC / Spike Base → Wall BreakBULL base case★★★★ 28%
Open / contextOpens 7,420–7,440; early two-way trade corrects into 7,403.50–7,409 (ON POC + spike base) and gets bought.
NarrativeValue builds overlapping-higher; midday acceptance over the 20-EMA, afternoon assault on 7,448.50–7,451.50; close above the wall targets 7,469.50.
SetupLong 7,405–7,410 · Stop 7,396 (under fib/polarity band) · T1 7,424.50 (+16) · T2 7,448.50 (+40) · T3 7,469.50 (+61) · R:R 1.3→4.7
Confirm / invalidConfirm: first hour holds ≥ 7,403.50. Invalid: 30-min close < 7,391.25 → G4.
G2 · 20-EMA Acceptance → Trend ContinuationBULL trend★★★ 20%
Open / contextOpens and holds above 7,436; shallow dips only to 7,424.50/7,421.50.
NarrativeCorrection formally over. Break of 7,451.50 runs the shelf ladder: 7,469.50 → 7,491.50; signing-confirmation headline extends toward 7,508/7,527.50.
SetupLong 7,452–7,455 on acceptance over ON HIGH (two 30-min closes > 7,448.50) · Stop 7,443 · T1 7,469.50 (+16) · T2 7,491.50 (+38) · T3 7,508.25 (+54) · R:R 1.6→5.4
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX < 20. Invalid: re-entry below 7,448.50 → look-above-and-fail, expect rotation to 7,424/7,409.
G3 · Double Rejection at the Wall → Range DayNEUTRAL fade★★★ 18%
Open / contextMorning tags 7,448.50–7,451.50 (second test) and rejects again, but 7,403.50–7,409 holds below.
NarrativeFriday bracket 7,391–7,451: fade the edges, value overlaps higher — constructive consolidation into the weekend, bulls keep the ball for Monday.
SetupShort 7,448–7,451 stop 7,458 → T 7,424.50/7,409; Long 7,405–7,410 stop 7,396 → T 7,424/7,440 · R:R 1.8→4.0 per rotation
Confirm / invalidConfirm: two failed wall-breaks by midday. Invalid: 30-min close outside 7,391–7,451.50.
G4 · Polarity-Band Failure → ON LOW / Deep RepairBEAR rotation★★ 15%
Open / context30-min close below ON VAL 7,391.25 — the only level set that flips the day.
NarrativeThrough the (now-tested) 7,383–7,387 band to ON LOW 7,374.25 / teal 7,366.25; if those fail, the deep-repair magnet is yVAH/fib 7,348.25–7,350 — a major responsive-buy ahead of the weekend.
Setup(a) Scalp short 7,389 stop 7,398 → T 7,374/7,366. (b) Core: long 7,349–7,353 or 7,365–7,368 on the responsive turn · Stop 7,340/7,358 · T1 7,374 · T2 7,391 · T3 7,409 · R:R up to 5.5
Confirm / invalidConfirm: orderly, VIX ≤ 21.5. Invalid for longs: 30-min close < 7,348 → G5.
G5 · Headline Unwind → Single-Print Fill → POC ZONEBEAR tail★★ 12%
Zone / contextNegative Iran headline (delay/re-escalation) breaks 7,348.25.
NarrativeThe open 7,337 single print fills, air pocket to the green zone 7,320 then the Jun-11 POC ZONE 7,294.25–7,307.50 (50-EMA) — weekend de-risking amplifies the move.
SetupShort 7,345 on loss of 7,348.25 · Stop 7,354 · T1 7,337 (+8) · T2 7,320 (+25) · T3 7,305 (+40) · R:R 0.9→4.4. Then responsive long 7,296–7,304 stop 7,288 if VIX < 23.
Confirm / invalidConfirm: headline + volume. Invalid: reclaim 7,366.
G6 · Friday-Afternoon De-Risk OverlayTIME-BASED★ 7%
Zone / contextConstructive day, but by 2 PM no signing confirmation.
NarrativeWeekend premium gets bought; longs trim from the wall back toward ON POC into MOC. A positioning fade, not a reversal — Monday gap risk remains two-sided on headlines.
SetupTrim to ¼-size core by 2 PM if unconfirmed; optional scalp short from 7,448–7,451 to 7,424/7,410, stop above HOD · R:R ~2.0
Confirm / invalidConfirm: VIX upticking while ES stalls. Invalid: signing confirmed → G2 resumes, stand down.

05Probability Matrix — updated

RankScenarioDirectionProb★
1G1 ON-POC dip buy → wall breakBull28%★★★★
2G2 20-EMA acceptance → 7,469/7,491Bull20%★★★
3G3 Wall double-rejection → range 7,391–7,451Neutral18%★★★
4G4 Polarity failure → deep repair 7,348–7,374Bear-rotation15%★★
5G5 Headline unwind → 7,337 fill → POC ZONEBear tail12%★★
6G6 2-PM de-risk overlayTime-based7%★
CombinedBull 48%Neutral 25%Bear 27%
Base case (updated)Open near the 20-EMA, an early rotation into 7,403.50–7,409 (ON POC + spike base) that gets bought, then a second assault on 7,448.50–7,451.50. The wall has rejected once — second tests break more often than not when value is migrating higher beneath them, but on an unsigned-deal Friday the break may not stick past 2 PM without confirmation. Net: buy the ON-POC dip, take profits generously at/through the wall, and respect the 2 PM clock.

06Decision Map — updated

FRIDAY RTH OPEN ~7,425–7,440 (20-EMA at price · wall 7,448.50–7,451.50 · ON POC 7,409)
│
├─► FIRST MOVE DOWN (rotation, no inventory owed)
│     ├─ holds 7,403.50–7,409 (ON POC + spike base)
│     │     └─ G1 LONG → 7,424.50 → 20-EMA → WALL 7,448.50–7,451.50
│     │            ├─ rejected 2nd time → G3 range fade 7,391↔7,451
│     │            └─ broken + held → G2 → 7,469.50 → 7,491.50
│     └─ 30-min close < 7,391.25 (ON VAL) → G4
│            └─ 7,383/7,387 → ON LOW 7,374.25 → 7,366.25
│                   ├─ BUY the turn 7,348.25–7,350 / 7,365–68 → back to 7,391/7,409
│                   └─ 7,348 fails (headline) → G5 → 7,337 fill → 7,320 → POC ZONE 7,294–7,307
│
└─► FIRST MOVE UP (drive)
      ├─ tags wall, rejects → fade to 7,424/7,409 → reassess as G1/G3
      └─ acceptance > 7,451.50 (2× 30-min closes) → G2 trend → 7,469.50 → 7,491.50
             └─ signing headline → 7,508 → 7,527.50/7,532

TIME RULE: 2 PM, no signing date → trim to ¼ core, tighten trails (G6)

Position size vs ATR — applies to all threeATR 105.5; ON used 77. 60–70% size, ¼-size after 2 PM without a signing confirmation, and no new full-size positions in the final hour of a pre-weekend headline tape. Shorts remain scalps only (wall fade G3, polarity scalp G4a, de-risk fade G6) while price is above 7,348 and VIX below 22.

09Structural Warnings — updated

⚠ The wall 7,448.50–7,451.50 — second test todayON already rejected there once. Second tests with rising value below usually break, but the break needs RTH volume and a 30-min close above to be tradeable — don't front-run it with full size on a Friday.
ℹ 7,387 single print FILLED — warning clearedThe overnight repaired the upper ladder and two-way traded 7,374–7,430. That zone is now legitimate support structure; the old "speed zone" caution between 7,383–7,396 is lifted.
⚠ 7,337 single print STILL OPENThe only untested air left below. It is irrelevant above 7,366 — but if 7,348.25 gives way, it fills fast and drags price toward 7,320/POC ZONE. Structure stops above it (7,340), never inside it.
⚠ 20-EMA at price at the bellThe open will print essentially on the daily 20-EMA (~7,434). Expect magnet chop around it in the first rotations — the cleaner trades are at the edges (7,403.50–7,409 below, 7,448.50–7,451.50 above), not in the middle.
⚠ Unsigned deal + weekend = the only real bear catalystPrice structure alone no longer supports a bear case above 7,348. The G5 path requires a headline. Equally: holding full-size longs over the weekend is a news bet — the 2 PM trim rule is the discipline line today.
ℹ New upper-stack roadmapYour new lines 7,565.00 / 7,578.25 / 7,591.50 / 7,603.50 (+fib cap) chart the path from 7,508/7,527.50 toward the ATH 7,632.25 — next week's map if the wall and 7,469/7,491 clear on a signed deal.

10Session Transition — Friday close → next week

If Friday closes at…ImplicationNext-week lean
> 7,469.50Wall + shelf cleared with valueBull continuation: 7,491 → 7,508 → 7,527.50/7,532; new lines 7,565–7,603.50 map the ATH approach on signing
7,451.50–7,469.50Wall broken, correction overBuy-dips regime; 7,448.50 = weekly pivot
7,391.25–7,451.50 (in ON value/range)Constructive weekend wait; value overlapping-higherSigning = gap up through the wall; delay = retest 7,374/7,348 first, still buyable
7,348.25–7,391.25Polarity lost into the weekend — caution wickMonday must reclaim 7,391 quickly or 7,337 fills
< 7,348.25News leg unwinding into the closePOC ZONE auction 7,294–7,307 Monday; bear template re-armed below 7,294

11Final Summary — RTH Friday Jun 12 (updated 06:29 ET)

ItemCall
Macro biasBULL — catalyst resolving, MA stack rising, value migrating up
Intermediate biasBULL — correction effectively over pending a daily close above the 20-EMA (~7,434–7,436)
Intraday biasBULL — buy ON-POC dips; shorts are scalps at the wall only
Base caseEarly rotation into 7,403.50–7,409 bought → second assault on 7,448.50–7,451.50 → break targets 7,469.50, with 2 PM trim if unsigned
Primary tradeLong 7,405–7,410, stop 7,396, targets 7,424.50 / 7,448.50 / 7,469.50 (G1)
Breakout tradeLong 7,452–7,455 after 2× 30-min closes > 7,448.50 → 7,469.50 / 7,491.50 / 7,508.25 (G2)
Deep-value alternateLong 7,349–7,353 if ON VAL fails and the repair completes → 7,374 / 7,391 / 7,409 (G4)
Key bull level7,451.50 (ON HIGH/wall) — acceptance unlocks 7,469.50 → 7,491.50 → 7,508+
Key bear level7,391.25 (ON VAL) — close below opens 7,374 → 7,366 → 7,348; and 7,348.25 is the headline trapdoor to the 7,337 fill/POC ZONE
Biggest riskWeekend headline binary on the unsigned deal — the 2 PM de-risk rule is non-negotiable today
Day-type expectationNormal-variation up / wall-break (≈48%) · range 7,391–7,451 (≈25%) · repair-then-recover (≈15%) · headline trend-down (≈12%)
ES RTH Plan · UPDATED Friday Jun 12, 2026 06:29 ET · Last 7,430.75 · ON: H 7,451.50 / L 7,374.25 / VAH 7,421.50 / POC 7,409.00 / VAL 7,391.25 · Spike base 7,403.50 (value-backed) · 7,387 print filled, 7,337 open · Iran deal unsigned — 2 PM de-risk rule active · Not financial advice. Verify VIX and final ON extremes at the bell.
